findings_none: full security review of the accumulated Phase D candidate — the epoch-1 inherited frontier (required-blocks.js + routing/contract surfaces) plus the epoch-2 8-file repair — traced every retired-hook resolution and manifest obligation and yielded zero candidate-caused security defects; the removal completes the Phase-C hook retirement without leaving a live path that resolves or executes an absent script and without weakening any containment the workflow relies on

## Review narrative

Digest binding (why I did not re-run the ~24-min suite): I recomputed computeLandableTreeDigest(cwd) via scripts/kaola-workflow-validation-runner.js and got da5911c85855c6dd0ea6dc324a8c4426603cfda6eaee47828023b3448ae63037, byte-identical to the gate candidate_digest, proving the working tree is unchanged since the recorded run. The receipt at kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/chain-receipt.json binds to exactly this candidate: base 1491c7e5, headSha a94ea0b7, codeTreeHash 9696b909a2034a2e1be4fce277bd8aafc97c04e0b2f6329337782a94e683d82b, scope all-four (reason edition_coupling), all four chains exitCode 0 (claude/codex/gitlab/gitea), and the five preamble checks (validate-script-sync, validate-vendored-agents, active-folders-parity, generate-routing-surfaces --check, edition-sync --check) all exit 0. opencode 384 + kimi 412 green are cited from the dispatch and corroborated by n1's captured $?. Re-running the chains is the exact redundant work this epic removes.

Security-focus check 1 — no dangling exec/readFileSync of a retired path (the security-relevant failure mode here):
- opencode (templates/opencode/plugins/kaola-workflow-hooks.js): the entire if (tool === "bash") { runHook(root, HOOK.preCommit, ...) ... return; } branch is removed, and HOOK.preCommit is dropped from the HOOK map. I confirmed no remaining call site passes undefined into hookPath, so path.join(root, ".opencode", "hooks", undefined) (the TypeError [ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE] that would have fired on every bash tool call had only the map entry been deleted) is now unreachable. The two surviving runHook calls pass the defined string constants HOOK.writeLane and HOOK.dispatchLog; and runHook (lines 70-83) fails open — hookPath returns null for an undeployed script and runHook returns {status:0} — so the surviving write-lane call against a now-retired script is inert, not a crash.
- kimi (scripts/sync-kimi-edition.js): HOOK_SCRIPTS now lists only kaola-workflow-subagent-dispatch-log.sh. writeHooks() (lines 516-532) iterates HOOK_SCRIPTS and readFileSyncs each; with the deleted kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh/kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh gone from the list, the prior ENOENT FATAL crash on the already-deleted canonical script is eliminated. HOOK_ADAPTATIONS no longer references write-lane and renderKimiHooksToml() emits only the two surviving [[hooks]] blocks. In the tests, the load-bearing write-lane end-to-end probe that spawnSynced .kimi/hooks/kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh (itself a would-be dangling exec of the deleted script) is removed from test-kimi-edition.js, and the test-opencode-edition.js H1 hookPath harness now targets kaola-workflow-subagent-dispatch-log.sh instead of the deleted pre-commit script (it still asserts fail-open out.missing === null, and hookPath is pure existsSync path-resolution, not an exec of the hook).

Security-focus check 2 — retirement is completion of the Phase-C decision, not a silent weakening; no other surface still wires the retired hooks:
- Canonical hooks/hooks.json carries only SessionStart (matcher compact -> codex-compact-resume) and SubagentStart (matcher * -> dispatch-log). There is no PreToolUse/Bash entry anywhere, so the wiring layer no longer resolves or executes pre-commit or write-lane in any edition.
- I grepped the four editions, hooks.json, and install/generator wiring for lingering pre-commit/preCommit/write-lane references. Every remaining hit is non-executable: code comments documenting the retirement (simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js:573, the walkthrough's no-PreToolUse assertions, docs/kimi-edition.md "The advisory pre-commit and write-lane hooks are retired..."), plan-validator comments where "pre-commit" denotes a validation phase rather than the hook, or operator-facing prose in next.skeleton.md/workflow-next.md/slots.js ("the pre-commit guard blocks ... multiple workflow project folders"). None of these resolve or spawn the deleted script. The prose is stale-documentation only: the operative agent instruction "Do NOT merge, interleave, or batch commits across folders" remains, and the single-project invariant enforcement moved to the sink/finalize transaction per the Phase-C decision (2a48342c), which predates the run base 1491c7e5 — so the staleness is a pre-existing, non-executable doc-accuracy condition, not a candidate-introduced weakening of a live containment. Write-lane's opencode surface is documented "dormant until enabled" and fail-opens; its manifest precondition never shipped. I admit no finding for the stale prose.

Security-focus check 3 — required-blocks.js (inherited epoch-1 security frontier) preserves manifest guard semantics:
- The module is consumed only by scripts/test-route-reachability.js (const { REQUIRED_BLOCKS } = require('../templates/routing/required-blocks.js')), which asserts, via foldsGeneric, that each block's content_tokens are present across the required routing surfaces. It is a pure positive-presence assertion manifest — it generates and injects nothing into any surface, so no injection into the generated routing surfaces is possible from this edit.
- Adding pr-mirror-before-dispatch adds a new positive obligation (the mirror-before-dispatch pin tokens must appear on all six plan-run surfaces) — a strengthening, and its content is task-list-mirror UI consistency prose, not a security-sensitive behavior. Removing fn-fast-compliance-backstop withdraws an obligation for a fast_compliance_unresolved pin tied to the retired fast path (D-725-01); with the fast path deleted there is no project for the backstop to fire against, so the removal retires a dead-feature obligation rather than weakening a live security-relevant checkManifest gate. The corresponding test-opencode-edition.js A20 assertion is retired in lockstep. Guard semantics preserved.

Security-focus check 4 — R1 re-pointed installer-rendering test pin: scripts/test-install-model-rendering.js changes a single assertion string from finalize.includes('\n\n## Validation Delegation Policy\n\n') to finalize.includes('\n\n## Steps\n\n'). It is a test-fixture blank-line-preservation probe over installed output; no runtime, filesystem, exec, or trust-boundary surface is touched.

Conclusion: the accumulated candidate is green over its digest-bound recorded validation, the epoch-2 hook retirement removes every dangling exec/readFileSync path and leaves no wiring that silently no-ops a retired guard, the required-blocks.js manifest keeps its presence-assertion semantics with no injection or weakened security obligation, and the R1 pin is inert. Zero admitted security blockers. Recording verdict pass, findings_blocking 0.

review_conclusion: Accumulated Phase D candidate certified with zero security blockers — retired-hook cleanup removes every dangling exec and readFileSync path in the opencode and kimi generators, canonical hooks.json wires no PreToolUse guard, required-blocks.js keeps its presence-assertion semantics with no injection, and the digest-bound receipt confirms all four chains green over this exact tree.
